arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta expected to bring two Arsenal players into his starting line-up vs Atalanta tonight - Sky journalist
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta is expected to bring Gabriel Jesus and Raheem Sterling into his Arsenal side to face Atalanta in the Champions League.

arts.ttl: ‘Wow’: Cesc Fabregas stunned after what he’s heard about Arsenal vs Tottenham
arts.sum: Arsenal beat Tottenham on Sunday in what wasn’t exactly a classic north London derby. Spurs vs Arsenal has been one of the Premier League’s most...

arts.ttl: Revealed: Full details from former Arsenal striker Jay Emmanuel-Thomas's court appearance over 'importing drugs' after 'partner' was arrested at Stansted Airport, as his bail application is rejected
arts.sum: Emmanuel-Thomas, a 33-year-old striker who currently plays for Scottish Championship side Greenock Morton, was arrested by National Crime Agency (NCA) officers earlier this week.

arts.ttl: Man City handed Kevin De Bruyne injury blow ahead of Arsenal clash
arts.sum: Manchester City star Kevin De Bruyne is a doubt for Sunday's huge Premier League clash with title rivals Arsenal after coming off at half-time against Inter on Wednesday. City and Arsenal are both still unbeaten at this stage of the Premier League season, with two points separating the pair in the current standings after four games.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola responds to Kevin De Bruyne injury scare as Man City star could miss Arsenal clash
arts.sum: Manchester City maestro Kevin De Bruyne faces a race against time to be fit for the titanic Premier League clash with Arsenal on Sunday, after picking up what looked to be a groin issue during the stalemate against Inter Milan on Wednesday. "Against a team that defended 5-3-2 and five players went to one side, they could not make contact with Rodri and you have to turn the ball quick and we needed players special for small spaces," he said.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals recovery timeline for 'quite significant' Martin Odegaard injury
arts.sum: Odegaard went down on international duty and the Norway team doctor initially revealed it would take at least three weeks for the Gunners captain to recover, with that timeline only likely to increase if more in-depth scans revealed further damage. Odegaard has missed just five Premier League games since joining Arsenal three years ago but will now double that tally at the very least, and Arteta warned his side they must adapt to life without the 25-year-old quickly.

arts.ttl: ‘Pretty sure’: Cesc Fabregas has now predicted the result of Man City vs Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal face Atalanta in the Champions League on Thursday, but most of the Gunners’ attention may already be on their next game. Indeed, Arsenal face...

arts.ttl: Jorginho responds to Man City and Liverpool claims ahead of Arsenal Champions League tie
arts.sum: Asked if players could ever strike or refuse to play, the influential Spain international said: "I think we are close to that. Meanwhile, Alisson shared a similar opinion and criticised the sheer number of games played in the football calendar.

arts.ttl: Fabregas makes 'too smart' Arteta admission in Man City vs Arsenal prediction
arts.sum: "I think you lose this weekend, it doesn’t have a big impact into the mind of the players, you know, going forward into the season, because there is still so many games to play. Arsenal legend Cesc Fabregas is optimistic Arsenal will come away with the three points at the Etihad Stadium when his old club faces Manchester City on Sunday.

arts.ttl: ‘This now sounds like’: Arsenal told how long Odegaard could be out for from someone with ‘experience’
arts.sum: Craig Burley has stated how long he believes Martin Odegaard will be sidelined for after suffering an injury similar to the Arsenal man.
